derivatives.name: umcsent
derivatives.definition: The raw University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment index — a monthly survey of US consumer confidence about personal finances and the economy.
derivatives.notes: Non-stationary in levels. Use derived transforms for regression and tournament signals.

derivatives.name: umcsent_yoy
derivatives.definition: The year-over-year percentage change in consumer sentiment — how much sentiment has risen or fallen compared with the same month last year.
derivatives.notes: WINNER signal (tournament code S2_yoy). Zero-crossing threshold (T4_zero). Approximately stationary.

derivatives.name: umcsent_mom
derivatives.definition: The month-over-month percentage change in consumer sentiment — how much sentiment moved compared with the prior month.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S3_mom. Approximately stationary.

derivatives.name: umcsent_zscore
derivatives.definition: The 36-month rolling z-score of consumer sentiment — how many standard deviations the current reading is from its recent average, with memory of the last 3 years.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S4_zscore. Stationary by construction. Key finding: countercyclical signal at extremes per interpretation_metadata.

derivatives.name: umcsent_3m_ma
derivatives.definition: The 3-month moving average of consumer sentiment — a smoothed version that reduces month-to-month noise.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S5_3m_ma. Non-stationary in levels; used as a regime indicator.

derivatives.name: xlv
derivatives.definition: The raw adjusted close price of the XLV ETF (Health Care Select Sector SPDR). All target-axis derivatives are computed from this single series.
derivatives.formula: raw column (Yahoo Finance, auto_adjust=True, monthly last close)

derivatives.name: xlv_ret_1m
derivatives.definition: The 1-month simple return of XLV — how much the ETF gained or lost in the current month.
derivatives.notes: Base return used in tournament performance calculations.

derivatives.name: xlv_ret_3m
derivatives.definition: The 3-month total return of XLV — cumulative price change over a quarter.

derivatives.name: xlv_ret_12m
derivatives.definition: The 12-month total return of XLV — one year of cumulative price appreciation, a long-horizon trend measure.

derivatives.name: xlv_vol_12m
derivatives.definition: The 12-month rolling realized volatility of XLV monthly returns, annualized — how volatile the ETF has been over the past year.
derivatives.notes: Risk metric for regime characterization. Decimal output (0.15 = 15% annualized).

notes: Wave 10B retro-apply: migrated from flat-array structure to schema-compliant axis_block format per QA-CL BLOCK. Controls (UNRATE, DGS10, VIX) remain out of scope per ECON-SD and are documented in interpretation_metadata.json.
